item.title: Guys, I don't see a ring on that finger.
item.dc:creator: WeirdAlisMySpiritUserName

item.title: Drawing swords... (Imgur Guide to the whole mess in Ukraine right now)
item.description:
Good afternoon Imgur. Over the weekend, the news was grim. The Russian Foreign Minister met with the US Secutary of State in tense talks beneath the cloudy skies of Geneva. Over 100,000 Russian troops sat ominously on the border of Ukraine. European and American leaders demanded sanctions and actions against Russia now, before the firing started... Wait? Why are we having a Cold War moment? Are we about to see war in Europe... again? This post will try and answer those questions. What is the situation, what is this going on, and why now? Got 15 minutes? I’ll try and cover this. Please note- while the guide will be as short as I can make it I’m gonna include so many sources the Baby Jesus himself will be like ‘Dude, that’s a lot of sources’. Reading them all could take up a lot of your day. Front Page Edit: I’m glad folks found this useful, and thank you usersub for the kind words and support.
So what do we know? What follows is a lot of news reports. A LOT is being said about this, and I’m am just gonna give you an raw information dump to begin with. Be warned, it could cause whiplash. Russia has about 100,000-130,000 or so troops from Military Department-West (and some from Military Department-South as well supposedly) near the Ukrainian border. This isn’t an planned exercise. To folks in Ukraine, the EU, NATO and the US? Best case it’s an attempt to intimidate Ukraine. Worst case? It’s an invasion force. According to the Congressional Research Service this build up is ‘significant... falling outside normal troop rotations or military exercises, due to the deployment of units over long distances and far away from their usual training grounds’. https://crsreports.congress.gov/product/pdf/IN/IN11806 Pictures show the size and scale of the military build up...
Such a force would literally be too large to stop. Ukraine doesn’t have the manpower and war ultimately is a numbers game. However, with the US allowing other NATO states send their US weapon systems to Ukraine (such as the Baltics) the aim seems to be to help Ukraine (NOT a member of NATO) by giving shiny new weapons which could allow them have a slightly better chance if a fight does start. https://www.politico.com/news/2022/01/19/us-allies-ukraine-weapons-russia-invasion-527375 Watching the events unfold over the past week has been interesting to say the least. We had the allegation by the United States that Russia was planting guys into Ukraine for the purpose of possibly attacking their own people and saying it was the Ukrainians who did it and thus giving Russia an excuse to go to war. That was a bit of a bombshell. https://www.nytimes.com/2022/01/14/us/politics/russia-ukraine-us-intelligence.html
Same video again... cos I be stupid.
Russia angrily refuted this allegation. Of course they did. https://www.usnews.com/news/business/articles/2022-01-17/russia-denies-looking-for-pretext-to-invade-ukraine But then America announced that Russia had recruited a bunch of Ukrainian politicians to take control of the country after a Russian take over... https://www.cnn.com/2022/01/20/politics/us-transfer-weapons-ukraine/index.html This huge deployment of 130,000 troops by the way isn’t totally new. I explained back on November 22nd- what triggered the current build up was simply the Ukrainians using a single Turkish drone to take out a single artillery post. https://www.chicagotribune.com/nation-world/ct-aud-nw-nyt-ukraine-groceries-20211116-qme4ouludjelddtlnzw6fchx3m-story.html
BUT over the last week, the tension has raised a lot and it isn’t about a drone strike anymore. Right now Russia says its about the growing influence of NATO. They want the West to say they will NEVER, E V E R let Ukraine join NATO. And to promise to never, ever expand NATO. And in fact to withdraw NATO from Eastern Europe. This explains why when high level NATO generals sat down in Vienna to meet their Russian counterparts last week the overall result was kinda like...
So, given the signs look like Russia is planning to attack, the British government have decided to supply a load of anti-tank weapons to Ukraine in the event a bunch of Russian tanks decide they want to come visit. https://www.wionews.com/world/uk-sends-2000-anti-tank-weapons-to-ukraine-amid-fears-of-russian-invasion-446457 But Germany are having important diplomatic talks with Russia and have said they want NOTHING to get in the way of the talks. And that means they would not even allow weapons intended for Ukraine to be flown over Germany air space. So the British flew around them. The transport planes flight paths were tracked and... well...
Edit: Someone mentioned in the comments that officially German didn’t ban the British from flying over their territory; rather they just didn’t ask and flew around them. Just an FYI. Correction and apology from the source of the above allegation: https://ukdefencejournal.org.uk/we-made-a-mistake-sorry/
Everyone is being kinda paranoid. Ukraine claimed the Russians were moving their people out of their embassy in Kiev (like you would do before an invasion), a story that the New York Times reported and others picked up on... https://thehill.com/policy/international/590124-russia-removing-staff-from-kyiv-embassy-report Recent reports say the US is now about to request diplomatic families also leave the country... https://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/jan/22/us-embassy-in-ukraine-requests-staff-evacuation-amid-war-fears Meanwhile the head of the CIA visited the Ukrainian President (again- the USA said its to talk about deescalating the situation; Russia gets all excited and says him turning up is ESCALATING the situation as ‘we are the victims here’) https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/792196.html
And when he arrived there was also a bunch of US Senators from both the Dems and the GOP turning up to say they stand with Ukraine... https://www.nbcnews.com/news/amp/ncna1287608 It should be said many sources have said the Ukrainian President told the US politicos that issuing sanctions AFTER the invasion is kinda the equivalent of saying ‘thoughts and prayers’ https://www.axios.com/ukraine-russia-sanctions-invasion-senators-9f657902-f4f9-40fe-a5e5-a6d58d4e1033.html
This was probably what Sec. State Blinken responded to when he said the US wouldn’t introduce sanctions against Russia unless they invaded... while also saying that a ‘single additional Russian force’ would trigger a US response. https://www.cnn.com/2022/01/23/politics/antony-blinken-russian-troops-ukraine-cnntv/index.html MEANWHILE... Canada has said they have sent a small group of Canadian Special Forces to Ukraine to ‘help’... https://www.ctvnews.ca/mobile/politics/small-group-of-canadian-special-forces-sent-to-ukraine-sources-confirm-1.5744027 And told its citizens to avoid Ukraine due to “ongoing Russian aggression”. https://nationalpost.com/pmn/news-pmn/crime-pmn/canada-asks-citizens-to-avoid-non-essential-travel-to-ukraine-due-to-russian-aggression
At the same time... The Union State (the official name of the fact that Russia and Belarus are legally speaking one nation (in a weird, ‘two nations, one cup’ deal) will be holding a joint Russian-Belarus military exercise to the north of Ukraine... https://www.upi.com/Top_News/World-News/2022/01/18/Belarus-Russia-Ukraine-Putin-Blinken/1441642521801/ Which has everyone now worried Russia is planning a two-pronged attack into Ukraine. https://www.politico.com/news/2022/01/18/blinken-europe-ukraine-russia-tensions-527257 (Why a two pronged attack- to split Ukrainian forces obviously). And that the exercises (due to start next month) could see Nuclear weapons being deployed into Belarus by Russia... https://www.france24.com/en/europe/20220118-us-fears-arrival-of-russian-troops-could-lead-to-nuclear-weapons-in-belarus
(More about this operation lower down) As you can see... There is a lot happening? So it’s war right? Maybe. Behind each statement and each position are lots of back story. Details that you need to know before you make up your mind about what’s gonna happen. So what is Putin doing?
Well, the theory amidst some, is that simply Russia has tried competing with the West and China when it came to ‘soft’ power. And has totally failed. China for example, dwarfs it and does its own thing (China isn’t interested in Russia’s claims on Ukraine- it has its own links with Ukraine https://www.fmprc.gov.cn/eng/zxxx_662805/202201/t20220104_10478830.html). And the West? Well a slew of former Soviet Union controlled nations are now independent and wish never again to be under Russian domination, so they joined the mutual defence alliance, NATO. Russia has tried everything in their soft power to prevent this. Diplomacy, sulks, threats... It didn’t work. So it appears as if Putin is using hard power to get his aims. If he can’t convince them by words... use force. It’s a theory anyway. One of MANY https://www.brookings.edu/blog/order-from-chaos/2022/01/11/around-the-halls-russia-ukraine-kazakhstan-and-the-european-security-order/
Now again and again we hear from Russia that NATO’s expansion is dangerous. However let’s put it like this: during the ACTUAL Cold War, both Sweden and Finland refused to join NATO. They kept a policy of strict neutrality. They saw no advantage in joining NATO. Now? Now in the face of Russia being so blatantly aggressive? They are debating joining NATO. Why wouldn’t they be? And of course Russia threatens retaliation if they do... https://sputniknews.com/20211224/nato-accession-of-sweden-and-finland-would-lead-to-adequate-response-from-russia-1091775676.html So while Russia is talking about Ukraine, as I said above, from their (possibly bullshit) POV it’s all about NATO.
It is worth noting NATO have accepted new members since 1997 but they NEVER deployed say US troops into the new member states so as not to antagonise Russia. Ever. Well until 2014 anyway. And the Russian invasion of Ukraine to seize Crimea. That’s when NATO began responding to requests from new members for help with training and stuff. This row has led to the ongoing ‘negotiations’ between NATO and Russia over military forces which could justify at least three long posts by themselves; Russia sees NATO as ‘America’ and really only wants to talk to the Americans but the issue with NATO is that it’s a lot of countries, all of whom are legally obligated to defend each other and changes in policy have to be agreed by ALL members to be ratified. So the Russians are trying to get America to negotiate a treaty about Europe without Europe being involved. Europe is having none of that so America cannot do so (even if they wanted to but they don’t). If you want to get lost in the intricate issues involved in the talks try this... https://www.brookings.edu/blog/order-from-chaos/2021/12/21/russias-draft-agreements-with-nato-and-the-united-states-intended-for-rejection/ Basically as one commentator said recently- Russia began the talks with NATO by issuing an ultimatum. And usually folks only begin talks with ultimatums so they can say the talks failed and use it as a pretext to go to war. https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/2021/12/21/putin-draft-treaties-negotiations-allies-mcfaul/
Side note about military doctrine: Last September Russia held the Zapad-2021 military exercise in the region facing Ukraine/Europe. Now what was interesting was what their huge ass war games in Zapad-21 showed. The Russians imagined they were being attacked from three differing directions intending on regime change in Belarus and/or Russia and so they trained for that. https://sgp.fas.org/crs/row/IF11938.pdf Militarily then the dogma is to believe the West will attack Russia and/or Belarus to overthrow their leaders. Now we who we live in the West? We know fricken disorganised we are. That ain’t happening. But it clearly is what is in the mind off Russia. Which leads us to the new war games planned with Belarus. https://apnews.com/article/moscow-russia-europe-belarus-ukraine-555703583c8f9d54bd42e60aca895590
Allied Resolve 2022. A joint Russia-Belarus military exercise to take place on the Northern Ukrainian border. It’s all about ‘defending themselves’. Supposedly from Ukraine (who are not in position to attack anyone). Extra troops will be deployed to the north of Ukraine beyond the 100,00 or so already deployed to the east. But these forces won’t be in place until February 9th and Allied Resolve supposedly takes place between February 10th to 20th. The dates are kinda significant. Remember them for a bit.
In Putin’s head it seems he wants to return to the days when Russia ran one part of Europe and America ran another part of Europe; but the world has moved on and countries are clearly deciding they want to enter a mutual defence agreement which prevents Russia from expanding its influence. So this is going to lead to war then right? Well... again no. I keep being drawn to the same news stories again and again and again... How calm it is in Ukraine and Russia. How skeptical the civilians on both sides are about the possibility of war happening. https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60090991 And https://amp.theguardian.com/world/2022/jan/10/its-our-house-mood-in-kyiv-calm-despite-threat-of-russian-attack And https://news.yahoo.com/with-russia-poised-to-invade-many-ukrainians-express-doubts-about-the-threat-221806711.html Why the sense of ‘it’s not gonna happen’ from the civilians on the ground? Why is the media hysteric but the folks who’d be on the front line not? What are we missing?
Well, is there anyone (who isn’t working for the Russian government and going ‘we are the victims here’ constantly) saying Russia won’t attack? A few. Oddly enough Time magazine leads this week with a cover story based on an interview with leading Russian dissident Alexai Navalny. 9 months ago I wrote about who he is and why he’s kinda important (link below). Allow me say- I’m not a fan of him personally (way to much blaming Russians problems on foreigners for my liking) but he IS the man who has successfully upset/wound up Putin more than any other in the world today. His interview is interesting. https://time.com/6140102/alexei-navalny-russia-profile/ The quotes that stood out for me? “Time and again the West falls into Putin’s elementary traps... It just takes my breath away, watching how Putin pulls this on the American establishment again and again.”
What is he on about? Well all this desire to prevent NATO from expanding and so forth is, according to Navalny, a smokescreen. ‘It is not what he [Putin] claims to be concerned about: the deployment of U.S. forces in Eastern Europe, or the chance that Ukraine might one day join the NATO alliance. “Instead of ignoring this nonsense,” Navalny writes, “the U.S. accepts Putin’s agenda and runs to organize some meetings. Just like a frightened schoolboy who’s been bullied by an upperclassman.” Ouch. Tell us how you really feel Alexai. The article says, ‘What Putin truly fears is what Navalny’s movement seeks—a change of power in Russia, followed by cashiering its corrupt clan of oligarchs and spies. It isn’t NATO that keeps Putin up at night; it’s the space for democratic dissent that NATO opens up along his border. This fear, Navalny argues, is what drives all the conflicts Russia wages with the West. “To consolidate the country and the elites,” he writes, “Putin constantly needs all these extreme measures, all these wars—real ones, virtual ones, hybrid ones or just confrontations at the edge of war, as we’re seeing now.”
And this is consistent with what basically all Russian opposition to Putin has been saying, and kinda what I’ve been saying since I first wrote a big ole post about this guy. Putin fears losing power. His entire state apparatus is designed to keep him IN power. That’s it. He isn’t the only totalitarian crook in charge of a nation in the region. Last year the moron in charge of Belarus faced protests who he immediately blamed on ‘foreign powers’ and then unleashed troops to come down upon them. And earlier this year when protests swept through Kazakhstan, killing scores of protestors and police, the President there issued a ‘shoot to kill’ order and then called for Russian help. The Russians sent thousands of troops from the Central Military District and they all just shot everyone until the protests ended. https://time.com/6137439/kazakhstan-protests-russia/ He also immediately blamed ‘foreign powers’.
So just so we understand each other- whenever democratic forces demand reform of these rather despotic nations? It’s foreigners. Usually us. At least according to these guys. Which brings us back to Putin. Vladimir Putin has a big problem. The last election was really bad for him. Now on paper it wasn’t bad at all. United Russia (his party) is way and by far the largest party in the Russian Duma having clocked up 49.82% of the vote and holding 324 seats (the nearest rivals, the old Communist party, holds 57 seats with 18.93% of the vote). How can this be said to be a disaster? https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-58614227.amp
Consider there are wide spread and documented cases of vast amounts of vote rigging; of false ballots, of ‘carousel voters’ (voters being bused around to vote many times) and more. Added to this Putin had jailed the main opposition leaders. And added to this Putin controls the media in Russia so his parties point of view is perpetuated 24/7. And even with THAT? He can’t even get 50% of the vote? All of that and he could only gain 49.82%? https://www.rferl.org/a/russia-election-fraud-shpilkin/31472787.html Seriously dude, the League of Dictators, Despots and Decidedly Evil Overlords are not even slightly impressed at those numbers. Those are Rookie Numbers.
Putin isn’t dumb. He knows he is unpopular. He needs to do something to regain a surge in support. Back with the annexation of Crimea? That worked for him. He gained a huge pop. He wants a huge pop. 2024 is the Presidential Election. Remember, Putin is somewhat venal. He wants to feel like he has won. So a small incursion in Ukraine to revive his flagging popularity? That makes terrible sense. Oh and while we are on the subject of Crimea?
The Russian Crimean Adventure Alright, so for all of my ‘Putin isn’t just gonna invade Ukraine...’ talk, we gotta face facts that he did. 2014. He invaded Crimea. The story doesn’t need to be gone into huge detail- if you want a pretty decent explanation of what went down read it here: https://www.brookings.edu/blog/order-from-chaos/2019/03/18/five-years-after-crimeas-illegal-annexation-the-issue-is-no-closer-to-resolution/amp/ However Crimea has become a hot mess for Russia. A total fricken disaster. Too harsh?
Consider. One quarter of the Crimean economy came from tourism. That collapsed. The place isn’t working. And the biggest issue is water. See Crimea got its water from... Ukraine. And as such when Russia took Crimea, Ukraine said ‘supply your own damn water’. It wasn’t much of a weapon. But it was surprisingly powerful. To compensate for the water issues alone Russia has spent over a five year period more money than it spent on its entire state healthcare system over the last three years. How bad is it? In the Soviet Era there were 400,000 hectares of arable land in the Crimea. Before the Russians took over inefficiency in the post-Soviet Ukrainian system had seen this drop to 140,000 hectares. One year after Russia took over? It’s down to 14,000 hectares. The Crimean agricultural industry is dead. And the same time millions of Russia’s moved to the Crimea; all filled with hope and national pride and... and... are now having to ration water. https://www.bloomberg.com/opinion/articles/2021-03-19/russia-vs-ukraine-crimea-s-water-crisis-is-an-impossible-problem-for-putin
Russia actually had the nerve to accuse Ukraine of trying to commit Genocide for doing this (aside from the audacity of invading a place and then sending the guys you kicked out the bill, it IS worth saying that technically under all international law- you occupy somewhere? You are responsible for it). So long side story short, Russia invaded Ukraine in 2014. They took Crimea. It made Putin more popular than he has ever been before. And then, like the Russia Olympic triumphs in the Winter Olympics of 2014? It turned out to be a dog turd covered in glitter spray. Like so much of what Putin promises. Crimea cost Russia a lot and that’s not even considering the price it had to pay because of sanctions imposed upon it after than and since then because they keep launching cyber attacks upon America. Oh and about the sanctions...
Last year I looked at the last bunch of sanctions Biden hit Russia with. How smart they were. How they left room to inflict genuine misery upon Russia if there were harsher. The main link to this assessment is here: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-04-15/u-s-sanctions-torpedo-russian-bonds-as-nuclear-option-planned?utm_source=url_link And basically, as I linked to last year, the Russian economy is screwed. https://www.project-syndicate.org/commentary/putin-economic-policy-and-sanctions-cripple-russian-economy-by-anders-aslund-2021-04 So harsher sanctions could hurt Russia a LOT.
So enough already... what is going to happen and why do I think it is happening now? The following is my assessment based on reading all of the above and simply trying to make sense of it all. I could be completely wrong. I probably am. But for what it’s worth... All politics is local. All. Forget the rhetoric, it always is. Putin has issues at home. His economy is in serious trouble. He faces widespread dissent towards his regime. He has had to lock up political opposition, curtail protests and clamp down hard. His standard playbook for years has been to create a foreign crisis for Russian to deal with to shore up support for him. Russians have become somewhat cynical about this- this is why we are seeing the lack of belief this current situation will amount to much amidst Russians. However Putin panders. Especially to older voters.
Back in 2018, faced with crippling costs to pay for them, he finally introduced pension reforms despite wide spread protests (the reforms while needed economically, were badly done https://carnegiemoscow.org/2018/10/09/putin-s-botched-pension-reform-pub-77451) and at least one party ran on a platform of rolling back these reforms during the last election https://www.reuters.com/world/russian-lawmakers-call-lower-pension-age-ahead-election-2021-04-07/). He needs older voters. They are a bedrock of support for him. The pension thing they don’t like. But what these older voters DO like is the memory of Russia dominating the world. They remember when it was the effective ruler of Eastern Europe. The pride in their strength. They remember that pride. Putin wants to give them that. This is why he wishes to expand Russian influence and wishes for a Europe where Russia dominates half. Why a mutual defence pact between democratic nations does threaten his dreams of delivering that.
And so he plays brinksmanship. The last time I wrote about Russia was after the last but one time he had 100,000 troops in and around the Ukraine border. This is the third time in a year he has done this to be precise. It does make everyone rush to calm down the situation and he looks like the big man with big dick energy. So it’s all bluff? Maybe. But based on the rhetoric this time it would be harder for him to back down without losing face it seems. But the thing is with Putin you just never underestimate him. Putin is gambling that Europe can’t afford sanctions against Russia... https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-01-09/europe-frets-about-economic-hit-if-russia-is-sanctioned-heavily
In some ways Putin has succeeded in exposing the realpolitik at the heart of the issue. All politics is domestic right? Germany isn’t 100% behind intervening in Ukraine. NATO is not totally united. The Eastern European nations are very concerned about Russia, some of the others less so, while some like Canada and the UK are quite militant. It is these divisions that give Putin wriggle room, and where he calculates the odds may be in his favour. However if all politics is domestic... what does this mean about the American response? The Biden administration is a post-Iraq War administration. America has lost its appetite for large scale intervention in places it seems. He has to run a very divided nation, but luckily here he is a veteran of dealing with Putin (much more formidable than the previous President, whose involvement in Ukraine is a low moment in US history).
The Americans do seem to be playing Russia the way they have been playing them since 2017. They don’t back down. They call out his agents and expose plans which is diplomatic speak for ‘look what we know about you; what are we NOT saying? What do we know that you don’t want us to say?’ It’s actually a fairly smart game. Blinken’s recent statement how even ‘one additional Russian force’ would trigger a response reinforces Biden’s comments a few days earlier about a limited Russian incursion (at the time many saw that as a Biden gaff; it now becomes clear he was referring to Germany and their position). And to be clear- the US has not said it will send troops into Ukraine. They won’t be doing. Period. What he said was a very specific threat- IF Russia goes into Ukraine then he will send about 5,000 into mostly the Baltic states (https://www.dailywire.com/news/breaking-biden-considering-sending-thousands-of-u-s-troops-ships-aircraft-to-eastern-europe) reminding Russia that America has been quite easy going about things. And this is combined with threatening to step up the sanctions (which as shown above, can have a genuinely crippling effect on the Russian economy), is quite a robust position all things considered. It’s a game of bluff and dare.
Does Putin risk it? Does he say expand the Donbass territory? Does he go all in knowing the costs of occupying Ukraine would be immense? Can he back down and save face? Can he back down and then do this again in a few months or has he spent all his political revenue on this crisis? Only time will tell. I am inclined to believe there will NOT be a huge war. Ukraine hasn’t attacked Russian forces and the US exposing the Russians potential for a fake attack upon them has rather reduced the effectiveness of any such claims from Russia. Putin can play for time however. He won’t have Forces in place for Allied Resolve until Feb 10th.
I think he is judging the situation. Carefully. I think he is calculating. Reading the comments by NATO leaders. And I think he has another incentive to hold off until the end of February. Invading Ukraine now will seriously piss off China. https://www.bloombergquint.com/politics/ukraine-crisis-beijing-olympics-may-affect-russia-s-vladimir-putin-s-thinking China doesn’t want the Olympic Games ruined by a war going on. They have refuted the allegation that they asked Putin NOT to invade, and I don’t think he’d obey them if they ever said that, but he doesn’t want to annoy them or make THEM lose face. Especially as he stuff planned with China. Let us not forget that Russia has also been grandstanding elsewhere... They are busy meeting with Iran to discuss the Iran nuclear deal... https://www.rferl.org/a/russia-iran-putin-raisi/31662000.html Which is why they are organising drills in the Indian Ocean (with Iran and China...) https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/russia-china-iran-hold-joint-074425974.html While operating drills right across the world apparently... https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/russia-to-hold-naval-drills-in-atlantic-pacific-arctic-and-mediterranean-2719906
It’s grandstanding. It’s not a build up to war. Russia can’t run operations on multiple fronts like that- especially as (and let me stress this) their ONLY aircraft carrier is broken (again) and can’t be fixed and they can’t afford to make a new one. But Putin doesn’t want to annoy China. So, cards on the table? I honestly believe Russia won’t attack between now and the Olympics start. It doesn’t need to. The closing ceremony takes place on February 20th when his joint Russian-Belarus military war games also end. He went into Crimea after the Sochi Olympics it should be noted. BUT if he delays his attack (if he’s gonna attack at all) into the Spring, the Spring thaw makes Ukraine something of a mud bath making any invasion more difficult. So IF he is gonna attack? It will be mid-February.
And I believe he is contemplating if he should risk an attack... or if he can claim victory from making the west dance to his tune again. If he can do the latter? He will back down and smile as he made us all panic like this. If he can’t? He may try for a limited incursion. And hope it doesn’t escalate while the Ukrainians use their new weapons to try and give him a bloody nose. But I’m just a little spod on Imgur. I only write this stuff down to help ME make sense of the world. I could be very wrong on this but for what it’s worth... I still don’t think he will risk it. He is a master of rattling sabres. That’s what I think he’s doing. I hope to God I’m correct.
Additional Sources: I just posted links to 48 sources for one story covering a range of news agencies from Russian ones to left leaning ones to right leaning ones and you want more? Sheesh. What are you? Suckers for pain? The Ukrainian president has issued a statement that was basically “Take a deep breath, calm down”... https://www.kyivpost.com/ukraine-politics/zelensky-urges-ukrainians-not-to-panic-and-has-message-for-close-allies.html The US State department has advised all Americans in Ukraine to basically leave... https://www.cnbc.com/2022/01/24/state-department-urges-us-citizens-to-leave-ukraine-amid-russian-tension.html But it is worth noting this advice AND the news they were evacuating families of embassy staff was NOT caused by “a specific change in the security situation... but the "totality" of the Russian military buildup and threatening posture toward Ukraine over the last several months”. https://www.axios.com/state-evacuating-diplomats-families-ukraine-2663edeb-ef2e-429b-afaf-71416fc34162.html Nine months ago I wrote about as much of the issues facing Putin back then just after the last time he had 100,000 troops on the border of Ukraine (wait, no it wasn’t the last time, it was the time before the last time). That has more sources. https://imgur.com/gallery/6mOjSa4 And nearly 3 years ago now I wrote my first huge ass post about Russia and its terminal decline under Putin. https://imgur.com/gallery/3vPzXMX And I did a post about the mess of Ukrainian politics, Joe Biden, Trump and more about two years ago now... https://imgur.com/gallery/Xu86rtk FYI- if you like what I do? You could always buy me a coffee. No obligations and certainly no contributions desired from folks who are students/broke. Look after you guys. https://www.buymeacoffee.com/Thefeck
item.dc:creator: thefeckamisigningupwithfacebook

item.title: All The Things He Can Hold
item.dc:creator: Devi8tor

item.title: Who likes videos?
item.dc:creator: Insomnia365

item.title: One of the first Native American bands, Redbone

item.title: 50 variations of Serotonin for your Monday morning
item.description:
You can hear it, right?
It does slap
She's a keeper
Asian parents showing off
Can someone name this song, I know it but from where?
The engineer has a factor of safety, but was it high enough?
That eye roll
Channel 5
She's Grandma, 120 yrs old
Have a day Here's the rest of the collection: http://imgur.com/gallery/5b5odbQ http://imgur.com/gallery/WseWyLN http://imgur.com/gallery/5RbEqE5 http://imgur.com/gallery/R4XfUed http://imgur.com/gallery/HDa9y3y http://imgur.com/gallery/KHBuDRq http://imgur.com/gallery/qWiNRSu http://imgur.com/gallery/NmRrPWO

item.title: I have too many memes (vol. 41) - a daily dump
item.description: Volume 41 of basically free* memes! Do whatever you want today. Today is your day. Be strong. Be fierce. Drink water from a stream. Enter a triathlon. Kick a toddler. Worship Satan on a mountain top. Steal cheese. Lick a softball. It's your day.
item.dc:creator: MCKnowsTooManyFactsAboutBees

item.title: Day 241: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Dos Sirenos Spicy Burger Beef Patty, Pepperjack, Jalapeno, Avocado, Chipotle Mayo, Onion Strings, Lettuce, Tomato From Dos Sineros Brewing In San Antonio, TX Thank You @thefuzziestofbutts For This Burger Submission! 10/10 Previous Day 240: https://imgur.com/gallery/mrTKzXA MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.title: A violation makes me feel violated
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas

item.title: Did you know imgur has mental health help built into the app?
item.description:
Go to your personal page
Click the widget thing upper right
Scroll down and select 'wellness'
Ta-da! Scroll for the suicide prevention hotline numbers in US and UK
Plus mental health tips
You can ask for help if you are concerned about a fellow imgurian
And internal resources of imgurians willing to talk. Please take care of yourself and reach out if you need help. You matter and it can get better.
item.dc:creator: theblankestblankintown

item.dc:creator: lucanus43

item.title: Col Puckett (94) pushes away his walker and stands at attention while rec. MOH
item.description:
Reportedly, when the President called Col Puckett to let him know he would be receiving the award, he responded, "Why all the fuss? Can't they just mail it to me?"
President Biden presents the Medal of Honor to retired U.S. Army Col. Ralph Puckett in the East Room of the White House on Friday. Alex Brandon/AP
South Korea's President Moon Jae-in and President Biden pose with Col. Ralph Puckett and his family. Citation: https://www.army.mil/medalofhonor/puckett
item.dc:creator: QuiteAmbassador

item.title: Amazon cares about your privacy
item.dc:creator: McAber

item.title: Funny text dump Pt 1
item.dc:creator: CarpathionWargasm

item.title: Grassley looked this old when I met him about 1997
item.description: When I met him at a town hall rally, he was going on against a minimum wage. He was talking to a business owner who said he couldn't afford to pay minimum wage (but couldn't afford to live in it himself). Grassley started whining about people getting cynical and then looked over and saw my "minimum wage for politicians" button and said, "I see some are already growing cynical." His smugness and self-assuredness of being totally right and better than everyone was overwhelming. Edit: Wow, I post something before going to bed and come on to find out it went viral. That's a pleasant turn of events.
item.dc:creator: DWolf
